question: Calculate the percentage of films with Action genre.  percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(film_id WHERE name = 'Action'), COUNT(film_id)), 100.0); action genre refers to name = 'Action' and category.category_id =  category_id = 1;
orig_question: Calculate the percentage of superheroes with blue eyes.  percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(id WHERE color = 'Blue'), COUNT(id)), 100.0); blue eyes refers to color = 'Blue' and color.id =  eye_colour_id = 7;
orig_masked: Calculate the percentage of [T9] with [V1] [C19].  percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM([C6] WHERE [C5] = '[V1]'), COUNT([C6])), 100.0); [V1] [C19] refers to [C5] = '[V1]' and [C6] =  [C19] = [V2];; [V1] is a value of the column [C5]; [V2] is a value of the column [C19]
evidence: percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(film_id WHERE name = 'Action'), COUNT(film_id)), 100.0); action genre refers to name = 'Action' and category.category_id =  category_id = 1;
orig_evidence: percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(id WHERE color = 'Blue'), COUNT(id)), 100.0); blue eyes refers to color = 'Blue' and color.id =  eye_colour_id = 7;
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T3.name = 'Action' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.film_id) FROM film AS T1 INNER JOIN film_category AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id INNER JOIN category as T3 ON T2.category_id = T3.category_id
orig_gold_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.colour = 'Blue' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.id) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id

question: Calculate the total items sold by employees whose age are 60 years old and below. age are 60 years old and below refers to SUBTRACT(datetime(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,'localtime'), datetime(birthdate) < 61)
orig_question: Calculate the total home team goal scored by players whose age are 30 years old and below. age are 30 years old and below refers to SUBTRACT(datetime(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,'localtime'), datetime(birthday) < 31)
orig_masked: Calculate the total [C94] [C94] by [T4] whose [C121] are [V3] [C121] and below. [C121] are [V3] [C121] and below refers to SUBTRACT(datetime(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,'[V1]'), datetime([C121]) < [V2]); [V1] is a value of the column [C121]; [V2] is a value of the column [C121]; [V3] is a value of the column [C121]
evidence: age are 60 years old and below refers to SUBTRACT(datetime(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,'localtime'), datetime(birthdate) < 61)
orig_evidence: age are 30 years old and below refers to SUBTRACT(datetime(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,'localtime'), datetime(birthday) < 31)
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(T3.quantity) FROM Employees AS T1 INNER JOIN orders as T2 ON T1.employeeid = T2.employeeid INNER JOIN orderdetails as T3 ON T3.orderid = T2.orderid WHERE datetime('2026-03-10', 'localtime') - datetime(T1.birthdate) < 61
orig_gold_sql: SELECT SUM(t2.home_team_goal) FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN match AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.away_player_1 WHERE datetime(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, 'localtime') - datetime(T1.birthday) < 31

question: How much is the average rating of the USA movies? USA refers to country = 'USA'; average rating refers to  AVG(rating)
orig_question: How much is the average build up play speed of the Heart of Midlothian team? Heart of Midlothian refers to team_long_name = 'Heart of Midlothian'; average build up play speed refers to  AVG(buildUpPlaySpeed)
orig_masked: How much is the average [C180] of the [V1] [T6]? [V1] refers to [C173] = '[V1]'; average [C180] refers to  AVG([C180]); [V1] is a value of the column [C173]
evidence: USA refers to country = 'USA'; average rating refers to  AVG(rating)
orig_evidence: Heart of Midlothian refers to team_long_name = 'Heart of Midlothian'; average build up play speed refers to  AVG(buildUpPlaySpeed)
gold_sql: SELECT Avg(T1.rating) FROM u2base as T1 INNER JOIN movies as T2 ON T1.movieid = T2.movieid WHERE T2.country = 'USA'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(t2.buildUpPlaySpeed) AS REAL) / COUNT(t2.id) FROM Team AS t1 INNER JOIN Team_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.team_api_id = t2.team_api_id WHERE t1.team_long_name = 'Heart of Midlothian'

question: Give the MovieID of movies with more than 5 but less than 7 rating. movies with more than 5 but less than 7 rating refers to rating >5 AND rating <7;
orig_question: Give the team_fifa_api_id of teams with more than 50 but less than 60 build-up play speed. teams with more than 50 but less than 60 build-up play speed refers to buildUpPlaySpeed >50 AND buildUpPlaySpeed <60;
orig_masked: Give the [C199] of [T7] with more than [V1] but less than [V2] [C180]. [T7] with more than [V1] but less than [V2] [C180] refers to [C180] >[V1] AND [C180] <[V2]; ; [V1] is a value of the column [C180]; [V2] is a value of the column [C180]
evidence: ovies with more than 5 but less than 7 rating refers to rating >5 AND rating <7;
orig_evidence: teams with more than 50 but less than 60 build-up play speed refers to buildUpPlaySpeed >50 AND buildUpPlaySpeed <60;
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT movieid FROM movie WHERE rating > 5 AND rating < 7
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT team_fifa_api_id FROM Team_Attributes WHERE buildUpPlaySpeed > 50 AND buildUpPlaySpeed < 60

question: Provide the location city of the employee who has Milgrom as their last name.  location city refers to location.locationcity where locationID = employee.locationID; Milgrom is the last name of employee;
orig_question: Provide the eye colour of the superhero who has Karen Beecher-Duncan as their full name.  eye colour refers to colour.colour where eye_colour_id = colour.id; Karen Beecher-Duncan is the full name of superhero;
orig_masked: Provide the [C5] of the [T9] who has [V1] as their [C20].  [C5] refers to [C5] where [C19] = [C6]; [V1] is the [C20] of [T9];; [V1] is a value of the column [C20]
evidence: location city refers to location.locationcity where locationID = employee.locationID; Milgrom is the last name of employee;
orig_evidence: eye colour refers to colour.colour where eye_colour_id = colour.id; Karen Beecher-Duncan is the full name of superhero;
gold_sql: SELECT T2.locationcity FROM employee as T1 INNER JOIN location AS T2 ON T1.locationid = T2.locationid WHERE T1.lastname = 'Milgrom'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.colour FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id WHERE T1.full_name = 'Karen Beecher-Duncan'

question: What is the dates of the inspections whose score is the highest in the normal range? dates refers to inspections.date; score is the highest in the normal range refers to MAX(score > 70 and score < 90);
orig_question: What is the examination date of the patient whose albumin is the highest in the normal range? examination date refers to Date; albumin is the highest in the normal range refers to MAX(ALB > 3.5 and ALB < 5.5);
orig_masked: What is the [C23] of the [T2] whose [C14] is the highest [C14]? [C23] refers to Date; [C14] is the highest [C14] refers to MAX([C14] > [V1] and [C14] < [V2]);; [V1] is a value of the column [C14]; [V2] is a value of the column [C14]
evidence: dates refers to inspections.date; score is the highest in the normal range refers to MAX(score > 70 and score < 90);
orig_evidence: examination date refers to Date; albumin is the highest in the normal range refers to MAX(ALB > 3.5 and ALB < 5.5);
gold_sql: SELECT date FROM inspections WHERE score = (SELECT score FROM inspections WHERE  score > 70 AND score < 90 ORDER BY score DESC)
orig_gold_sql: SELECT Date FROM Laboratory WHERE ALB > 3.5 AND ALB < 5.5 ORDER BY ALB DESC LIMIT 1
note: Fixed a missing link to date

question: Provide the country affiliation of the canoeing athlete who is 226 cm tall. 226 cm tall refers to height = 226; canoeing athlete refers to sport_name = 'Canoeing'; country affiliation refers to region_name where region_id = noc_region.id;
orig_question: Provide the hair colour of the human superhero who is 185 cm tall. 185 cm tall refers to height_cm = 185; human superhero refers to race = 'human'; hair colour refers to colour where hair_colour_id = colour.id;
orig_masked: Provide the [C5] of the [V1] [C17] who is [V2] cm [C23]. [V2] cm [C23] refers to [C23] = [V2]; [V1] [C17] refers to [T8] = '[V1]'; [C5] refers to [C5] where [C22] = [C5].[C6];; [V1] is a value of the column [C17]; [V2] is a value of the column [C23]
evidence: 226 cm tall refers to height = 226; canoeing athlete refers to sport_name = 'Canoeing'; country affiliation refers to region_name where region_id = noc_region.id;
orig_evidence: 185 cm tall refers to height_cm = 185; human superhero refers to race = 'human'; hair colour refers to colour where hair_colour_id = colour.id;
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T6.region_name FROM person as T1 INNER JOIN competitor_event as T2 ON T1.id = T2.competitor_id INNER JOIN event as T3 ON T2.event_id = T3.id INNER JOIN sport as T4 ON T3.sport_id = T4.id INNER JOIN person_region as T5 ON T1.id = T5.person_id INNER JOIN noc_region as T6 ON T5.region_id = T6.id WHERE T4.sport_name = 'Canoeing' and T1.height = 226
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.colour FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.race_id = T2.id INNER JOIN colour AS T3 ON T1.hair_colour_id = T3.id WHERE T1.height_cm = 185 AND T2.race = 'Human'
note: Fixed link of superhero to the table

question: How many feminine actors born in 1990 were in a Comedic film? List them by ID. feminine refers to gender = 'Female'; born in 1990 refers to YEAR(Date of Birth) = 1990; in a Comedic film refers to genre = 'Comedy'
orig_question: How many female patients born in 1964 were admitted to the hospital? List them by ID. female refers to SEX = 'F'; born in 1964 refers to YEAR(Birthday) = 1964; admitted to the hospital refers to Admission = '+'
orig_masked: How many [C64] [T3] [C59] in [V1] were [C58]? List them by [C63]. [C64] refers to [C64] = '[V3]'; [C59] in [V1] refers to YEAR([C59]) = [V1]; [C58] refers to [C58] = '[V2]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C59]; [V2] is a value of the column [C58]; [V3] is a value of the column [C64]
evidence: feminine refers to gender = 'Female'; born in 1990 refers to YEAR(Date of Birth) = 1990; in a Comedic film refers to genre = 'Comedy'
orig_evidence: female refers to SEX = 'F'; born in 1964 refers to YEAR(Birthday) = 1964; admitted to the hospital refers to Admission = '+'
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.actorid FROM actor as T1 INNER JOIN characters as T2 ON T1.actorid = T2.actorid INNER JOIN movie as T3 ON T3.movieid = T2.movieid WHERE T3.genre = 'Comedy' AND T1.Gender = 'Female' AND STRFTIME('%Y', "Date of Birth") = '1983'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT ID FROM Patient WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', Birthday) = '1964' AND SEX = 'F' AND Admission = '+'

question: For the actors with an abnormal height, how many of them are in comedic films? abnormal height refers to Height (Inches) > = 65; in comedic films refers to genre = 'Comedy';
orig_question: For the patient with an abnormal alkaliphophatase level, how many of them are admitted to the hospital? abnormal alkaliphophatase level refers to ALP > = 300; admitted to the hospital refers to Admission = '+';
orig_masked: For the [T3] with an [C15], how many of them are [C58]? [C15] refers to [C15] > = [V1]; [C58] refers to [C58] = '[V2]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C15]; [V2] is a value of the column [C58]
evidence: abnormal height refers to Height (Inches) > = 65; in comedic films refers to genre = 'Comedy';
orig_evidence: abnormal alkaliphophatase level refers to ALP > = 300; admitted to the hospital refers to Admission = '+';
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT (DISTINCT T1.actorid) FROM actor as T1 INNER JOIN characters as T2 ON T1.actorid = T2.actorid INNER JOIN movie as T3 ON T2.movieid = T3.movieid WHERE T3.genre = 'Comedy' AND T1."Height (Inches)" >= 65
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.ALP >= 300 AND T1.Admission = '+'

question: Please list the titles of the books with no advance and an abnormal number of authors. no advance refers to advance = 0 ; abnormal number of authors refers to au_ord > 1;
orig_question: Please list the IDs of the patients with no thrombosis and an abnormal level of creatinine phosphokinase. no thrombosis refers to Thrombosis = 0 ; abnormal level of creatinine phosphokinase refers to CPK < 250;
orig_masked: Please list the [C32] of the [T1] with no [C13] and an [C20]. no [C13] refers to [C13] = [V2] ; [C20] refers to [C20] < [V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C20]; [V2] is a value of the column [C13]
evidence: no advance refers to advance = 0 ; abnormal number of authors refers to au_ord > 1;
orig_evidence: no thrombosis refers to Thrombosis = 0 ; abnormal level of creatinine phosphokinase refers to CPK < 250;
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.title FROM titles as T1 INNER JOIN titleauthor as T2 ON T1.title_id = T2.title_id WHERE T2.au_ord > 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.ID FROM Laboratory AS T1 INNER JOIN Examination AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.Thrombosis = 0 AND T1.CPK < 250
